opp.name: Viaero Wireless_SRM+P2P_May'26
opp.next_step: Bailey: To send ROI business case form Preyanksha: Follow up with Ellen for a pre-demo session post her internal review with CFO
opp.ais_why:
* No customer-expressed AI interest: the only AI reference is rep-side and hedged - "Zycus platform includes AI capabilities embedded in every module to assist users, which can be toggled off if undesired."
* The buyer's needs are process automation, not AI: contract spend-utilization tracking, PO/invoice automation with approvals, and supplier risk monitoring.
* The deal is parked: "current projects delay immediate evaluation; suggests follow-up in a few months," with materials to be reviewed by CEO/CFO. Qualified at 10%; Medium confidence.

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026 (Ellen Powell)
SF Next_Step__c (current): Bailey to send ROI business case form
SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c: empty
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or financial impact stated by the buyer across the single discovery call (14 May) or any Salesforce field. Ellen Powell articulated general interest in procurement digitization and mentioned 'big projects in the works' as the reason for deferral, but provided no cost savings target, cycle-time reduction goal, headcount impact, error-rate baseline, or ROI threshold. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c fields are all empty. The deal owner committed to sending an ROI business case form to Ellen (Next Step, current), but no buyer-stated metrics exist to anchor it. Without a quantified value case co-created with the buyer, the $100k amount is placeholder. The highest-leverage move is to run a structured value-discovery session with Ellen and ideally the CFO to surface and quantify the operational pain (manual PO processing, invoice errors, supplier onboarding delays, maverick spend) and establish a joint ROI model before any demo.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ellen Powell, Influencer (not Champion or Decision Maker)
Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026: Ellen initiated contact, expressed interest, stated she would review with CFO/CEO
No evidence of active championship: zero CFO/CEO introductions, zero internal docs shared, zero urgency created, zero competitive defense
Ellen deferred evaluation 14 May and has been silent for 34 days (last activity 1 June)
SF Next_Step__c: Preyanksha to follow up post-CFO review (no evidence review occurred)
champion.narrative: Ellen Powell (Director of Supply Chain, Influencer contact role) is the single point of contact and was positioned as an internal advocate on the 14 May discovery call—she reached out to Zycus, expressed interest, and stated she would review internally with the CFO and CEO. However, she has demonstrated no active championship behaviors: she has not introduced the deal team to the CFO or CEO, has not shared internal documents or timelines, has not driven urgency, and has not pushed back on the deferral she herself initiated. After stating 'check back in a few months' on 14 May, she went silent; the Next Step indicates Preyanksha will follow up with her post-CFO review, but there is no evidence the CFO review occurred or that Ellen is actively working the deal internally. Her role is Influencer, not Decision Maker, and she has no confirmed access to or sponsorship from the Economic Buyer (Mike Felicissimo, CFO). Without a champion who can navigate the internal approval process, introduce us to power, and create urgency, the deal will remain stalled in Qualified. Ellen's engagement is cooling (34 days silent), and her championship is weak. The move is to re-engage Ellen with a value proposition that helps her make the internal case (ROI model, peer references, analyst validation), AND to bypass her via executive connect to the CFO to test whether executive sponsorship exists independent of Ellen's advocacy.

sources: SF Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c: empty
SF How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c: empty
Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026 (Ellen Powell): no competitor, incumbent, or RFP shortlist mentioned
No tasks, emails, or Next Step references to competitive activity
competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are empty, Ellen Powell made no mention of other vendors or an RFP shortlist on the 14 May discovery call, and How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (current stack) and Existing_vendor__c are empty. There is no evidence of whether Viaero Wireless is running a multi-vendor RFP, sole-sourcing to Zycus, evaluating an incumbent renewal (e.g. SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua), or addressing the problem with homegrown tools or manual processes. The absence of competitive intel at Qualified stage is a discovery failure: we do not know what we are being compared against, what the buyer's current-state pain with their existing approach is, or how to differentiate. The deferral Ellen initiated ('check back in a few months') may indicate they are prioritizing another vendor evaluation or an internal IT project. Until we know the competitive set and the current-state solution, we cannot position differentiation or defend our wedge. The move is to ask Ellen directly in the re-engagement call: 'What are you using today for procurement and P2P? Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus? Is there an RFP or shortlist?' and to surface the incumbent or competitive dynamic so we can tailor our positioning and demo to win on our strengths (AI/Merlin, integration, user experience, services).

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026, Ellen Powell: general interest in procurement modernization, but 'right now is probably not the best time, we've got some big projects in the works'
SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c: empty
No quantified pain, no named pain owner, no compelling event
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain articulated by the buyer. Ellen Powell expressed general interest in procurement and supply chain modernization on the 14 May discovery call but provided no concrete pain points: no manual process bottlenecks described, no supplier onboarding delays quantified, no invoice error rates stated, no maverick spend problem disclosed, no ERP integration pain surfaced. She immediately deflected to timing: 'right now is probably not the best time, we've got some big projects in the works', which implies competing priorities are the primary blocker, not urgent procurement pain. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, and What_if_this_is_not_done__c are all empty. Without confirmed pain owned by a named executive (CFO or CPO), the deal has no urgency and no compelling event. The 'big projects in the works' Ellen referenced are unknown and may be higher-priority IT or business initiatives that crowd out procurement digitization. The move is to run a pain-discovery session with Ellen, IT, and Finance to surface and quantify the cost of the status quo (manual PO cycle time, invoice discrepancies, supplier relationship friction, audit risk) and identify the executive who owns that pain and would sponsor the fix.

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026 (Ellen Powell, no contracting or procurement discussion)
SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c: empty
No MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec, or SI discussion in any call, task, or field
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, legal review timeline, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or SI/partner involvement disclosed. The deal is at Qualified stage with no demo held, no pricing shared, no MSA/SOW discussion, and no procurement workflow mapped. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty, so there is no incumbent contract forcing function. Ellen Powell's 14 May call was interest exploration only; no discussion of how Viaero Wireless procures software, whether they require a multi-round RFP, whether InfoSec and IT governance reviews are mandatory, or whether an SI (e.g. Deloitte, Accenture) would be involved in implementation. The Next Step indicates Zycus will send ROI materials and follow up for a pre-demo session, but contracting and procurement are not on the radar. Until the buyer confirms their procurement process (direct purchase vs RFP, legal review SLA, InfoSec questionnaire requirements, implementation partner preference), we cannot timeline the paper process or de-risk it. The move is to ask Ellen directly in the re-engagement call: 'Walk us through how Viaero procures enterprise software—RFP required? InfoSec review? Typical legal timeline?' and map the end-to-end workflow so we can resource it and timeline it accurately.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Mike Felicissimo, Decision Maker
Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026, Ellen Powell: 'CFO and CEO are the ultimate decision makers'
SF Next_Step__c: 'Follow up with Ellen for a pre-demo session post her internal review with CFO' (no evidence review occurred)
Zero CFO engagement across calls, tasks, emails (52 days)
economic_buyer.narrative: Mike Felicissimo, Executive Vice-President and Chief Financial Officer, is assigned the Decision Maker contact role, and Ellen Powell stated on the 14 May discovery call that 'CFO and CEO are the ultimate decision makers'. However, Mike has never engaged: zero calls, zero emails, zero tasks, and the promised CFO internal review (Ellen committed to review with CFO post-14 May call) produced no follow-up or confirmation. There is no evidence Mike controls budget for this initiative, no pricing or commercial conversation has occurred, and no procurement approval workflow has been disclosed. The CFO's silence after 34 days, combined with Ellen's explicit deferral ('right now is probably not the best time'), signals either that the initiative lacks CFO sponsorship or that Ellen has not successfully positioned it internally. Until Mike (or the CEO) engages directly, budget authority remains unconfirmed and the deal cannot progress beyond Qualified. The move is an executive connect: the deal owner's manager should reach Mike directly to confirm strategic fit, budget availability, and timeline, bypassing the single mid-level thread.

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026, Ellen Powell: 'CFO and CEO are the ultimate decision makers'
SF Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c: empty
Call sequence: one discovery call (14 May), zero follow-ups, zero escalations, zero new participants in 52 days
decision_process.narrative: Ellen Powell stated on 14 May that 'CFO and CEO are the ultimate decision makers', establishing a two-executive approval gate, but no further process detail was disclosed: no procurement workflow, no IT/InfoSec review stage, no legal contracting timeline, no committee structure. The call sequence itself (one discovery call, zero follow-ups, zero escalations, zero new participants) shows a stalled process, not an active one. Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c, and What_is_the_decision_process__c fields are empty. The buyer explicitly deferred the evaluation ('check back in a few months'), and the promised Ellen-to-CFO internal review produced no output or re-engagement, suggesting either that Ellen has not formally initiated the process internally or that the CFO deprioritized it. Until the CFO and CEO engage and the buyer discloses their procurement and approval workflow, the process remains opaque. The move is to secure a three-way call (Ellen, CFO, Zycus exec) to confirm whether a formal evaluation is approved, surface the internal approval chain, and map the procurement steps (RFP, legal, InfoSec, contracting) required to close by Nov 2026.

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026 (Ellen Powell, high-level exploration)
SF Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c: empty
No RFP, RFI, or shortlist disclosed; no demo held
decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, or weighted scorecard disclosed by the buyer. The 14 May discovery call with Ellen Powell was high-level interest exploration, not a requirements-definition session; Ellen mentioned general interest in procurement and supply chain digitization but provided no must-haves, nice-to-haves, or evaluation dimensions. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are empty. The deal is at Qualified stage with no demo scheduled, no RFI/RFP in flight, and no shortlist disclosed, so criteria may not yet be formalized on the buyer side. However, the absence of any stated evaluation framework after 52 days, combined with the deferral and silence, suggests the buyer has not prioritized this initiative enough to define requirements. The move is to run a structured discovery workshop (ideally with Ellen, IT, and Finance) to co-create the decision criteria, map them to Zycus capabilities, and surface any gaps before a demo, so we control the criteria definition rather than reacting to an RFP we did not shape.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — No AI appetite signal from buyer. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are all empty. Ellen Powell made no mention of AI, automation, machine learning, or intelligent workflows on the 14 May discovery call; her interest was general procurement and supply chain digitization. AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c is empty, indicating no RFP or formal AI requirements. Without a buyer-stated AI need or strategic initiative, AI fit is unknown. However, the product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSupplier) overlaps with Merlin/I2O agentic use cases (intelligent invoice matching, supplier negotiations, spend analytics), so latent fit may exist but has not been surfaced. The move is to introduce the Merlin narrative in the re-engagement: position AI-driven procurement automation as a competitive differentiator and efficiency lever, and test whether it resonates with Ellen or the CFO as a strategic priority.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — No AI appetite signal from buyer.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning strength cannot be assessed; no AIS score, no AI discussion on the 14 May discovery call, and no AI-related fields populated (AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c empty). Ellen Powell's interest was general procurement modernization with no mention of AI, automation, or Merlin capabilities. The product scope (iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing per SF Products__c) includes areas where Merlin/I2O agentic capabilities could differentiate (intelligent invoice matching, supplier negotiation agents, procurement analytics), but the AI value proposition has not been introduced to the buyer. Given the silence and lack of engagement, AI positioning has not occurred and is under-positioned by default.
bullets.text: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning strength cannot be assessed; no AIS score, no AI discussion on the 14 May discovery call, and no AI-related fields populated…

bullets.full: No real champion yet — No strong champion identified. Ellen Powell (Director of Supply Chain, Influencer) is the only engaged contact and was positioned as an internal advocate, but she has demonstrated weak championship: she deferred the evaluation on 14 May, has not introduced the deal team to the CFO or CEO, has not created urgency, and has been silent for 34 days. Her role is Influencer, not Champion or Decision Maker, and she has no confirmed access to the Economic Buyer (CFO Mike Felicissimo). Without an active champion who can navigate internal approval, introduce us to power, and drive the timeline, the deal remains stalled and at risk of going dark. Champion-building is the top priority: re-engage Ellen with a compelling value proposition and executive validation, AND secure an executive connect to the CFO to test whether sponsorship exists independent of Ellen's advocacy.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 8 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15); momentum 52 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls). Eased toward flat — 34d quiet (>30d window); momentum down 3pt (stalling).
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.49).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 38 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (8), nudged by commitment 38 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls).

AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c is empty, indicating no RFP or formal AI requirements. Without a buyer-stated AI need or strategic initiative, AI fit is unknown. However, the product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSupplier) overlaps with Merlin/I2O agentic use cases (intelligent invoice matching, supplier negotiations, spend analytics), so latent fit may exist but has not been surfaced. The move is to introduce the Merlin narrative in the re-engagement: position AI-driven procurement automation as a competitive differentiator and efficiency lever, and test whether it resonates with Ellen or the CFO as a strategic priority.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 14 May 2026 in Qualified stage and has remained there for 52 days with zero stage progression. Last Salesforce activity 1 June (task or event), 34 days ago. Buyer explicitly deferred evaluation on 14 May ('check back in a few months') and has been silent since early June. No field-history stage changes, no amount changes, no close-date slips recorded. Next Step updated 18 June (last modified date) to reflect open commitments (Zycus to send ROI form, Preyanksha to follow up post-CFO review), but no evidence those actions occurred or that Ellen re-engaged. Deal is cooling without executive engagement or confirmed evaluation timeline.

If CFO does not sponsor this initiative, deal cannot progress beyond Qualified.

items.action: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager reaches CFO Mike Felicissimo directly (phone + LinkedIn) to confirm strategic fit, test executive sponsorship independent of Ellen's advocacy, surface budget availability and timeline, and secure a three-way call (CFO, Ellen, Zycus exec) to re-initiate the evaluation. Bypass the single mid-level thread and create top-down urgency.
items.trigger: CFO named as ultimate decision maker by Ellen Powell but has never engaged in 52 days; no CFO meeting, no budget discussion, no commercial conversation. Deal cannot progress beyond Qualified without CFO sponsorship.
items.expected_effect: Confirms or disproves CFO sponsorship within 7 days. If CFO engages, unlocks budget discussion, multi-threading, and progression to the relevant stakeholder CFO is unresponsive or disengaged, clarifies that deal should be downgraded to Omitted and resources redeployed. Highest leverage to break 34-day silence and test whether Nov 2026 close is realistic.

items.action: Re-engage Ellen Powell with a value-driven outreach (not a check-in): send the ROI business case form Bailey committed to (Next Step), attach 2-3 relevant CFO/CPO peer case studies (procurement cost reduction, cycle-time improvement, supplier performance gains), and a Gartner or Forrester 2026 procurement digitization report positioning Zycus. Frame as 'Here are the materials to help you make the internal case to your CFO; when can we schedule 20 minutes to walk through the ROI model together and confirm next steps?' Set a 7-day response deadline.
items.trigger: Ellen Powell silent for 34 days since 1 June; Next Step indicates Bailey to send ROI form and Preyanksha to follow up post-CFO review, but no evidence actions occurred or Ellen re-engaged. Ellen is the only engaged contact and the single thread; if she does not respond, deal is dark.
items.expected_effect: Tests whether Ellen is still engaged and willing to advocate internally. If she responds, provides materials to help her sell internally and opens door to CFO meeting. If she does not respond within 7 days, confirms deal is cooling/dark and triggers executive connect (rank 1) as the only remaining path. Expected to clarify engagement status within 10 days.

items.action: Run a structured value-discovery and pain-quantification workshop with Ellen (and ideally IT, Finance) to surface and quantify the operational pain (manual PO processing cost, invoice error rate, supplier onboarding delays, maverick spend %, audit risk) and co-create a joint ROI model anchored to Viaero's current-state metrics. Position the workshop as 'no-obligation exploration to see if there's a fit' (low-threat given the deferral) and use it to surface the pain owner, the compelling event, and the internal priority relative to the 'big projects in the works' Ellen referenced.
items.trigger: No quantified business case or metrics stated by buyer in 52 days; Customer_Business_Problem__c, Value_to_Customer__c empty. Ellen expressed general interest but no concrete pain. $100k amount is placeholder. Cannot build urgency or defend forecast without buyer-stated value case.
items.expected_effect: Surfaces and quantifies the pain, identifies the pain owner (CFO, CPO, CIO), and creates a shared ROI model to anchor the business case. If successful, converts general interest into a compelling event and provides Ellen (and Zycus) ammunition to justify CFO engagement. Expected to move deal from cooling to active discovery within 20 days if Ellen agrees to participate.

items.action: Surface the competitive landscape and current-state solution: in the re-engagement call with Ellen, ask directly: 'What are you using today for procurement and P2P—homegrown, ERP-native, or a vendor solution? Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus, or is this a sole-source exploration? Is there an RFP or shortlist in play?' Capture the incumbent, the competitive set, and the buyer's current-state pain with their existing approach so we can tailor the demo and positioning to win on our differentiation (AI/Merlin, integration, user experience).
items.trigger: Competitive landscape entirely unknown; Competitors__c, Existing_vendor__c empty; no incumbent, RFP shortlist, or current-state solution disclosed on 14 May discovery call. Cannot position differentiation or defend wedge when competitive set is invisible.
items.expected_effect: Clarifies competitive context within one call. If sole-sourced, strengthens our position and allows us to shape criteria. If multi-vendor RFP or strong incumbent, surfaces the differentiation battle and informs demo content, pricing strategy, and Merlin positioning. Expected to inform next-step demo and competitive strategy within 20 days.

items.action: Introduce the Merlin/I2O AI-driven procurement narrative in the re-engagement and position it as a strategic differentiator: 'Viaero's CFO and CEO are likely prioritizing cost efficiency and operational leverage—our Merlin agentic procurement capabilities (intelligent invoice matching, supplier negotiation agents, spend analytics) deliver measurable ROI and competitive advantage that legacy procurement platforms cannot. Would a 30-minute AI procurement demo with your CFO resonate as a strategic conversation?' Test whether AI automation is a buyer priority and use it to create urgency and executive interest.
items.trigger: No AI discussion on 14 May discovery call; AIS_Score__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c empty. Product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSupplier) overlaps with Merlin use cases but AI value proposition has not been introduced. AI positioning is under-positioned and may be the wedge to re-engage CFO.
items.expected_effect: Tests whether AI/Merlin narrative resonates as a strategic priority for CFO/CEO and creates differentiation vs legacy procurement vendors. If CFO is AI-curious or under board pressure to deploy AI, this may unlock executive engagement and fast-track evaluation. Expected to surface AI appetite and inform demo/positioning strategy within 20 days.

items: Only 1 buyer call in 52 days (discovery 14 May); zero follow-up calls, zero demos, zero executive engagement
Buyer explicitly deferred evaluation 14 May: 'right now is probably not the best time, we've got some big projects in the works... check back in a few months'
34 days since last Salesforce activity (1 June); no buyer email, call, or task since early June
CFO Mike Felicissimo (Economic Buyer) has never engaged despite being named ultimate decision maker; no CFO meeting occurred
Single-thread to Ellen Powell (Influencer, not DM or Champion); no IT, Finance, Legal, or Procurement leadership involved
No quantified business case, no metrics, no compelling event, no ROI threshold stated by buyer
No competitor, incumbent, or RFP shortlist disclosed; competitive landscape unknown
No demo scheduled, no formal evaluation initiated, no progression toward Shortlisted or Vendor Selected in 52 days
confidence_signals.summary: Confidence signals are weak and cooling. Only one discovery call (14 May) in 52 days; buyer immediately deferred evaluation and has been silent for 34 days (last activity 1 June). No demo scheduled, no CFO meeting, no multi-threading, no progression beyond Qualified. Single-threaded to Ellen Powell (Influencer) who has not re-engaged despite stated intent to review with CFO. No quantified business case, no competitive intel, no formal evaluation timeline. Deal is cooling without executive sponsorship or confirmed buyer commitment.

north_star_verdict.math: 144 days to close (26 Nov 2026). Created 14 May 2026, 52 days old, entirely in Qualified stage. No submission-date milestones recorded. Last activity 1 June (34 days ago). Buyer explicitly deferred on 14 May stating 'right now is probably not the best time, we've got some big projects in the works... check back in a few months'. No CFO meeting occurred despite Ellen's stated intent to review internally. At current pace (zero progression in 52 days, zero milestone advancement, zero executive engagement) the deal cannot reach Vendor Selected by mid-year 2027, let alone close Nov 2026. Required pace: move to Formal Evaluation within 30 days, secure CFO meeting within 45 days, reach Shortlisted by Q4 2026. Verdict: At Risk due to cooling engagement (34 days silent, last real touch 1 June) plus single-thread contributor.
evidence: Last Salesforce activity 1 June 2026, 34 days ago; no buyer call, email, or task since

Ellen Powell (Influencer, not DM) explicitly deferred evaluation 14 May: 'right now is probably not the best time, we've got some big projects in the works... check back in a few months' (discovery call 14 May 2026)
CFO Mike Felicissimo (Economic Buyer per contact role) has never engaged; no CFO meeting occurred despite Ellen stating she would review internally with CFO and CEO
Zero calls discovered after initial 14 May discovery call; no demo, no follow-up, no multi-threading
Next Step indicates Zycus to send ROI form (Bailey) and Preyanksha to follow up with Ellen post-CFO review, but no evidence CFO review happened
Single-threaded to Ellen Powell; no IT, Finance, Legal, Procurement leadership engaged
No budget discussion, no pricing, no commercial conversation in 52 days since creation
north_star_verdict.headline: 34 days since last buyer touch, CFO engagement never materialized, single-threaded to mid-level influencer who explicitly deferred evaluation in May and has gone silent since early June; deal is cooling without executive sponsorship or confirmed budget

flags: Quantified value case is missing and blocks CFO engagement. Ellen articulated qualitative pain (workflow delays, no spend tracking, supplier inquiries, fragmentation) but no financial impact stated: no cost-of-inaction, no savings target, no cycle-time baseline, no headcount reduction. The ROI form promised on 14 May has not been delivered (Next Step 18 Jun confirms still open). A data-backed business case quantifying savings (e.g. AP cycle time reduction, maverick-spend capture, supplier inquiry reduction) is the single highest-leverage artifact to move Ellen's internal brief from 'interesting' to 'must-evaluate' and secure CFO calendar time.

Competitive landscape is unknown at Qualified stage, a discovery failure. No competitor named, no RFP shortlist disclosed, no incumbent identified. Ellen described fragmented current state but named no vendors. Discovery calls are the moment to surface the shortlist; the 14 May call was Zycus-led demo, not competitive probing. Before the pre-demo session, run competitive discovery: ask Ellen directly who else is being considered, whether this is sole-source or multi-vendor, and what incumbent platforms (contract mgmt, invoicing, requisition) are being replaced or augmented. Positioning differentiation (single-line-of-code architecture, AI/Merlin, analyst leadership) requires knowing who we are being compared to.

CFO gate blocking progression: Ellen stated CFO and CEO are decision makers but CFO has never engaged; no CFO meeting, no budget discussion, no commercial conversation. Without CFO sponsorship, deal cannot move beyond Qualified.

No quantified business case: Zero metrics, no cost savings target, no ROI threshold, no compelling event. $100k amount is placeholder. Cannot defend forecast or build urgency without buyer-stated value case.
Competitive blindness: No competitor, incumbent, or RFP shortlist disclosed. Cannot position differentiation or tailor demo when competitive set is invisible. Discovery failure.

No formal evaluation timeline: Buyer deferred indefinitely ('check back in a few months'); no demo scheduled, no RFP in flight, no shortlist stage. Nov 2026 close date is not anchored to buyer timeline.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal exhibits multiple high-severity gaps blocking progression from Qualified: (1) single-threaded to mid-level Influencer with no Decision Maker or Economic Buyer engagement, (2) buyer explicitly deferred evaluation and has been silent for 34 days, (3) no quantified business case or metrics, (4) competitive landscape unknown, (5) no formal evaluation criteria or process disclosed, (6) no budget discussion or CFO meeting despite Ellen's stated intent. Forecast is indefensible (Pipeline at Qualified, 144 days to close, zero executive engagement, cooling for 34 days). Deal is at risk of going dark without immediate executive-level re-engagement.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape entirely unknown. No competitor named in Salesforce fields, no incumbent disclosed, no RFP shortlist mentioned on the 14 May discovery call, and no current-state solution or vendor stack surfaced. Cannot position differentiation, tailor demo, or defend wedge when competitive set is invisible. This is a discovery failure at Qualified stage and a knowledge gap that blocks effective positioning.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning strength cannot be assessed; no AIS score, no AI discussion on the 14 May discovery call, and no AI-related fields populated (AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c empty). Ellen Powell's interest was general procurement modernization with no mention of AI, automation, or Merlin capabilities. The product scope (iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing per SF Products__c) includes areas where Merlin/I2O agentic capabilities could differentiate (intelligent invoice matching, supplier negotiation agents, procurement analytics), but the AI value proposition has not been introduced to the buyer. Given the silence and lack of engagement, AI positioning has not occurred and is under-positioned by default.

items.quote: Ellen Powell expressed interest in procurement and supply chain modernization
items.source: Avoma discovery call 14 May 2026; SF Products__c: iSupplier;eProcurement;eInvoicing

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No formal evaluation criteria or stated expectations disclosed by buyer, so fit cannot be assessed. Ellen Powell expressed general interest in procurement and supply chain digitization (14 May call) but provided no requirements, must-haves, or evaluation dimensions. Products in scope (iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing per SF Products__c) align with typical mid-market procurement digitization, but buyer has not confirmed functional priorities, integration requirements, AI appetite, or implementation timeline expectations. Fit is inferred as aligned based on product-category match, but unconfirmed without a requirements discussion.

hard.ais_why:
* No customer-expressed AI interest: the only AI reference is rep-side and hedged - "Zycus platform includes AI capabilities embedded in every module to assist users, which can be toggled off if undesired."
* The buyer's needs are process automation, not AI: contract spend-utilization tracking, PO/invoice automation with approvals, and supplier risk monitoring.
* The deal is parked: "current projects delay immediate evaluation; suggests follow-up in a few months," with materials to be reviewed by CEO/CFO. Qualified at 10%; Medium confidence.

value.flag: Quantified value case is missing and blocks CFO engagement. Ellen articulated qualitative pain (workflow delays, no spend tracking, supplier inquiries, fragmentation) but no financial impact stated: no cost-of-inaction, no savings target, no cycle-time baseline, no headcount reduction. The ROI form promised on 14 May has not been delivered (Next Step 18 Jun confirms still open). A data-backed business case quantifying savings (e.g. AP cycle time reduction, maverick-spend capture, supplier inquiry reduction) is the single highest-leverage artifact to move Ellen's internal brief from 'interesting' to 'must-evaluate' and secure CFO calendar time.

value.flag: Competitive landscape is unknown at Qualified stage, a discovery failure. No competitor named, no RFP shortlist disclosed, no incumbent identified. Ellen described fragmented current state but named no vendors. Discovery calls are the moment to surface the shortlist; the 14 May call was Zycus-led demo, not competitive probing. Before the pre-demo session, run competitive discovery: ask Ellen directly who else is being considered, whether this is sole-source or multi-vendor, and what incumbent platforms (contract mgmt, invoicing, requisition) are being replaced or augmented. Positioning differentiation (single-line-of-code architecture, AI/Merlin, analyst leadership) requires knowing who we are being compared to.
